message:
test(output): add 30+ tests for 0% coverage functions, push package from 80.3% to 86.8% (bd-32jrj)

Cover Formatter.Output (JSON/text/error branches), Formatter.ErrorWithHint,
Formatter.Print, ProgressMsg.{Warningf,Errorf,Infof,Printf}, PrintSuccess/f,
PrintWarning/f, PrintInfo/f, Operation.{Start,Summary}, PrintSuccessCheck,
PrintSuccessFooter, SuccessFooter, FormatCLIError plain-text branches, and
Progress() constructor.

Remaining 0% functions are untestable: confirm.go (interactive stdin),
Fatal/FatalWithHint/FatalMsg (os.Exit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2unr","title":"Unit Tests: BV integration and recommendation parsing","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the BVClient module that integrates with bv --robot-triage.

## Test Cases

### Parsing Tests
- Parse valid bv --robot-triage JSON output
- Handle empty recommendations list
- Handle malformed JSON gracefully
- Extract PageRank, UnblocksCount, BlockedBy correctly

### Error Handling Tests
- bv binary not found
- bv returns non-zero exit code
- bv output timeout
- Invalid workspace path

### Integration Tests
- Call real bv with test workspace
- Verify recommendation order matches bv output
- Test --limit parameter
- Test caching behavior

## Logging Requirements
Each test should log:
- Test name and purpose
- Input parameters
- Expected vs actual output
- Pass/fail with detailed reason on failure

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-2usu","title":"Fix status bar to render exactly once at fixed bottom position","description":"## Problem
The status bar (Fleet/Alerts/Beads/Mail/Ckpt/Bugs) is included in the main scrollable content area instead of being rendered in a fixed footer region.

## Screenshot Evidence
Multiple "Fleet: 0/17 (C:16) Alerts: 5w Beads: R:0 Mail: offline Ckpt: none Bugs: --" lines appearing at the bottom of the dashboard.

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2067-2070 (ticker panel rendering)
- The tickerPanel.View() is written directly to the strings.Builder without height constraints

## Root Cause
The View() function at line 1972 builds content sequentially:
1. Header (banner + session)
2. Stats bar
3. Main content (pane grid)
4. Ticker bar (line 2069)
5. Quick actions (line 2075)
6. Help bar (line 2084)

When total content exceeds terminal height, the ticker/help bars get duplicated as old frames remain visible.

## Fix Required
1. Create a fixed footer region that renders independently of main content
2. Use lipgloss height constraints to ensure footer is always at the bottom
3. Implement a layout manager that allocates:
   - Fixed header region (top)
   - Scrollable content region (middle, fills remaining space)
   - Fixed footer region (bottom)

## Implementation Pattern
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) View() string {
    // Fixed regions
    header := m.renderHeader()   // ~6 lines
    footer := m.renderFooter()   // ticker + help, ~3 lines
    
    // Calculate available height for content
    headerH := lipgloss.Height(header)
    footerH := lipgloss.Height(footer)
    contentH := m.height - headerH - footerH
    
    // Render and constrain content
    content := m.renderMainContent()
    contentView := lipgloss.NewStyle().
        Height(contentH).
        MaxHeight(contentH).
        Render(content)
    
    // Assemble with explicit positioning
    return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,
        header,
        contentView,
        footer,
    )
}

func (m Model) renderFooter() string {
    var b strings.Builder
    b.WriteString(m.tickerPanel.View() + "\n")
    if qa := m.renderQuickActions(); qa != "" {
        b.WriteString(qa + "\n")
    }
    b.WriteString(styles.GradientDivider(m.width-4, ...) + "\n")
    b.WriteString(m.renderHelpBar())
    return b.String()
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status bar appears exactly once at the bottom
- [ ] Resizing terminal doesn't cause duplication
- [ ] Footer remains visible even with long content
- [ ] Scrolling main content doesn't affect footer

## Unit Tests
1. TestFooter_RendersOnce - footer content appears exactly once in View()
2. TestFooter_PositionedAtBottom - footer is at terminal bottom regardless of content
3. TestFooter_WidthFillsTerminal - footer spans full width
4. TestFooter_ContentOverflow - long content doesn't push footer off-screen

## E2E Tests (VHS)
1. Record dashboard at 80x24 - verify footer visible
2. Record dashboard at 200x50 - verify footer at bottom
3. Record resize sequence - verify no footer duplication

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log footer height calculation
- DEBUG: Log content truncation when exceeding available height","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyValley","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:20.731532847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:54.038793113Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:54.038745753Z","close_reason":"Fix already implemented in View() function with proper header/footer separation and truncateToHeight(). Tests TestViewFitsHeightAndFooterOnce and TestViewFitsHeightWithManyPanes verify the fix.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2usu","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2usu","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2usu","depends_on_id":"bd-3v69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2v63z","title":"Implement BV triage integration","description":"Implement BV triage integration.

## Functionality

Fetch and parse bv --robot-triage output to get prioritized beads.

## Implementation

```go
func getBvTriage() (*BvTriageOutput, error) {
    out, err := exec.Command("bv", "--robot-triage").Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    var triage BvTriageOutput
    json.Unmarshal(out, &triage)
    return &triage, nil
}
```

## Data Needed From BV

- recommendations[].id - bead ID
- recommendations[].title - bead title
- recommendations[].unblocks_count - how many items it unblocks
- recommendations[].status - current status
- recommendations[].effort - estimated effort

## Error Handling

- If bv not available: return helpful error
- If triage empty: inform user no beads found
- If bv fails: include stderr in error","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:01.195512751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:58:21.030321639Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:58:21.029682806Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete. BV triage integration is fully functional with GetTriage(), caching, and helper functions in internal/bv/triage.go. Checkpointing already exists. Extend it to capture **assignment/progress context** so restores are more useful for multi‑agent work.

## Scope
- Include current assignments (bead → agent) in checkpoint metadata
- Include BV snapshot summary (ready/blocked counts) at checkpoint time
- Ensure robot outputs (`--robot-checkpoint-*` if present) include these fields
- Keep existing checkpoint storage format backward‑compatible (additive fields)

## Tests
- Unit tests for new checkpoint fields (serialize/deserialize)
- E2E: create checkpoint during active assignments; restore; verify summary emitted

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-CHECKPOINT] session=%s assignments=%d ready=%d blocked=%d", …)

- New fields present when assignment tracking is enabled

 {"id":"bd-32svm","title":"Task: Wire checkpoint export/import --redact-secrets to shared engine","description":"## Scope
Ensure checkpoint export/import uses the canonical redaction engine instead of maintaining separate regex lists.

## Context
A prior checkpoint export/import implementation introduced `--redact-secrets` with an embedded regex list. We want a single source of truth.

## Requirements
- Preserve CLI flags and behavior (`--redact-secrets`) for compatibility.
- Internally delegate to the shared redaction engine + config.
- Ensure manifest/checksum logic isn’t impacted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate regex list remains in checkpoint export code.
- Unit tests cover at least one end-to-end export redaction case.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:57.777780055Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:56.140520893Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:56.140326Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-r6d5e","title":"E2E Tests: ntm spawn --assign full workflow","description":"## Test Coverage
Full end-to-end test of ntm spawn --assign convenience flag.

## Test Scenario
1. Create test workspace with beads
2. Run ntm spawn --assign (single command)
3. Verify agents spawned
4. Verify beads assigned
5. Verify prompts received by agents
6. Verify combined output

## Test Script (tests/e2e/test_spawn_assign.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG=/tmp/ntm-e2e-spawn-assign-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log
exec > >(tee -a $LOG) 2>&1

echo "[INFO] Starting ntm spawn --assign E2E test at $(date)"

# Setup
echo "[STEP 1] Creating test workspace..."
TEST_DIR=/tmp/ntm-spawn-assign-test-$$
mkdir -p $TEST_DIR
cd $TEST_DIR
br init
for i in {1..4}; do br create "Test bead $i" --type task; done
echo "[OK] Created 4 test beads"

# Spawn + Assign in one command
echo "[STEP 2] Running ntm spawn --assign..."
RESULT=$(ntm spawn ntm-spawn-assign-$$ --cc=2 --no-user --assign --strategy=round-robin --json)
echo "[DEBUG] Result: $RESULT"

# Verify spawn
echo "[STEP 3] Verifying spawn..."
AGENT_COUNT=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.spawn.agents | length')
if [ "$AGENT_COUNT" -ne 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected 2 agents, got $AGENT_COUNT"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] 2 agents spawned"

# Verify assign
echo "[STEP 4] Verifying assignments..."
ASSIGNED=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.assign.assigned | length')
if [ "$ASSIGNED" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "[FAIL] Expected at least 2 assignments, got $ASSIGNED"
    exit 1
fi
echo "[OK] $ASSIGNED beads assigned"

# Cleanup
echo "[STEP 5] Cleaning up..."
ntm kill ntm-spawn-assign-$$
rm -rf $TEST_DIR
echo "[OK] Cleanup complete"

echo "[PASS] ntm spawn --assign E2E test passed at $(date)"
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp each step
- Log combined output
- Log spawn and assign phases separately
- Save full log to /tmp

 {"id":"bd-r85h","title":"E2E Tests: Staggered spawn and prompt delivery","description":"E2E Tests for Staggered spawn and prompt delivery.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic staggered spawn
   - Spawn 5 agents with --stagger=5s
   - Verify ~5s between each spawn
   - Verify all eventually running

2. Staggered prompt delivery
   - Spawn session, send prompt to all with stagger
   - Verify delivery times
   - Verify order preserved

3. Adaptive staggering
   - Enable rate limit detection
   - Trigger rate limit
   - Verify stagger increases
   - Verify recovers when clear

4. Dependency-aware stagger
   - Tasks with dependencies
   - Staggered assignment
   - Verify blockers complete before blocked

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-STAGGER] Spawning agent N/TOTAL at TIMESTAMP
[E2E-STAGGER] Interval: ACTUAL (base=BASE, adaptive=ADJUSTMENT)
[E2E-STAGGER] Rate limit detected, increasing delay to NEW_DELAY
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Spawn timing verified within 500ms tolerance
3. Prompt delivery order preserved
4. Adaptive staggering increases on rate limit
5. Dependency-aware ordering correct
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-STAGGER] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify staggered spawn
2. Agent launches respect stagger delay
3. Prompt delivery respects timing
4. Rate limit avoidance verified
5. Test measures actual spawn intervals
6.  {"id":"bd-r9fy","title":"Implement global animation tick source for synchronized animations","description":"## Problem
Multiple animated components with independent tick counters can cause visual noise.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Dashboard Already Uses Global Tick

**Central tick source exists:**
- `dashboard.go:42-43` - `DashboardTickMsg` message type  
- `dashboard.go:224` - `animTick int` in Model
- `dashboard.go:745` - Single 100ms tick: `tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*100, ...)`
- `dashboard.go:1599-1600` - Handler increments `m.animTick++`

**All animations use the global tick:**
```go
// dashboard.go:2618 - progress bar uses m.animTick
barContent := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent/100.0, barWidth, "█", "░", m.animTick, colors...)

// dashboard.go:2142 - ticker panel receives animTick
m.tickerPanel.SetAnimTick(m.animTick)

// layout.go:238 - layout functions receive tick parameter
bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent/100, width-4, "█", "░", tick, ...)

// dashboard.go:2718 - spinner uses animTick
statusIcon = WorkingSpinnerFrame(m.animTick)
```

### Orphaned Code in components/progress.go

The `components/progress.go` file has UNUSED independent tick mechanisms:
- Line 132: 50ms tick for ProgressBar
- Line 166: 50ms tick for IndeterminateBar
- Line 224: 50ms tick for IndeterminateBar.Init()

**These are NOT used by the dashboard.** The dashboard uses `styles.ShimmerProgressBar()` directly, which takes a tick parameter.

## Updated Fix Approach

### 1. Clean Up Orphaned Code
```go
// components/progress.go - Consider deprecating or removing the independent tick
// since dashboard uses styles.ShimmerProgressBar() with global tick instead

// Option A: Deprecate
// Deprecated: Use styles.ShimmerProgressBar() with global tick instead
func (p ProgressBar) Init() tea.Cmd { ... }

// Option B: Remove entirely if unused
```

### 2. Document Global Tick Pattern
Add to CONTRIBUTING.md:
```markdown
## Animation Synchronization

All animations in the dashboard use a global tick source:

1. The dashboard maintains `animTick int` incremented every 100ms
2. Pass `animTick` to all animated rendering functions
3. Never create independent tea.Tick sources in subcomponents
4. Use `styles.Shimmer()`, `styles.ShimmerProgressBar()` with tick parameter

Example:
\`\`\`go
// Good: use global tick
bar := styles.ShimmerProgressBar(percent, width, "█", "░", m.animTick, colors...)

// Bad: independent tick in subcomponent
func (m MyModel) Init() tea.Cmd {
    return tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*50, ...) // Creates separate timer
}
\`\`\`
```

### 3. Add Test for Tick Synchronization
```go
func TestAllAnimationsUseGlobalTick(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify that styles.Shimmer produces deterministic output for given tick
    result1 := styles.Shimmer("test", 42, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    result2 := styles.Shimmer("test", 42, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    
    if result1 != result2 {
        t.Error("Shimmer should be deterministic for same tick value")
    }
    
    // Different ticks produce different results
    result3 := styles.Shimmer("test", 43, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    if result1 == result3 {
        t.Error("Different ticks should produce different shimmer effects")
    }
}
```

## Verification Checklist
- [x] Dashboard uses single DashboardTickMsg source (100ms)
- [x] animTick passed to all animated components  
- [ ] Remove/deprecate orphaned tick code in components/progress.go
- [ ] Document global tick pattern in CONTRIBUTING.md
- [ ] Verify NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1 disables all animation

## Priority Adjustment
Changed to P3 - cleanup/documentation task.  {"id":"bd-rcwdt","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-synthesize for triggering synthesis","description":"# Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-synthesize for triggering synthesis

Trigger Stage 3 synthesis for an ensemble session via robot mode.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION \
  --strategy=manual|adversarial|consensus|creative|analytical|deliberative|prioritized|dialectical|meta-reasoning|voting|argumentation-graph \
  --format=markdown|json|yaml \
  --output=/path/to/report.md \
  --force
```

## Behavior
1. Validate ensemble session exists and outputs collected
2. If outputs incomplete and `--force` not set, return error
3. Run OutputCollector + Synthesizer + DisagreementAuditor
4. Emit deterministic formatted output (markdown/json/yaml)
5. Return audit summary + output path

## Response Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "action": "ensemble_synthesize",
  "session": "arch-review-2024-01-15",
  "status": "complete",
  "strategy": "adversarial",
  "report": {
    "summary": "...",
    "output_path": "/tmp/ensemble_report.md",
    "findings_count": 12,
    "recommendations_count": 7
  },
  "audit": {
    "conflict_count": 5,
    "unresolved": 2,
    "high_conflict_pairs": ["A1↔E2"]
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- ENSEMBLE_NOT_FOUND
- SYNTHESIS_NOT_READY (outputs incomplete)
- OUTPUT_SCHEMA_INVALID
- INTERNAL_ERROR

## Logging Requirements
- Log start/end time and strategy
- Log number of outputs collected + invalid outputs
- Log audit conflict counts

## File Location
internal/robot/ensemble_synthesize.go

## Dependencies
- OutputCollector (bd-1av7i)
- Synthesis system (bd-2qwm8)
- DisagreementAuditor (bd-38go8)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:41:04.554611187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:58:19.853721727Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:58:19.853638883Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-ensemble-synthesize command with strategy, format, output, and force options","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rcwdt","depends_on_id":"bd-1av7i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rcwdt","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rcwdt","depends_on_id":"bd-38go8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
